heading: Review of discharge characterization
label: 2534 Review of discharge characterization

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The decision to conduct a case review under this section shall be at the discretion of the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating.

children.heading: .—Board of review
children.label: 2 .—Board of review
children.text: In addition to the requirements of , a board of review for a former member of the Coast Guard established pursuant to such section and under part 51 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of enactment of the Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2025), may upon a motion of the board and subject to review by the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating, downgrade an honorable discharge to a general (under honorable conditions) discharge upon a finding that a former member of the Coast Guard, while serving on active duty as a member of the armed forces, committed sexual assault or sexual harassment in violation of section 920, 920b, or 934 of title 10 (article 120, 120b, or 134 of the Uniform Code of Military Justice).section 1553 of title 10

children.heading: .—Evidence
children.label: 3 .—Evidence
children.text: Any downgrade under paragraph (2) shall be supported by clear and convincing evidence.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 4 .—Limitation
children.text: The review board under paragraph (2) may not downgrade a discharge of a former member of the Coast Guard if the same action described in paragraph (2) was considered prior to separation from active duty by an administrative board in determining the characterization of discharge as otherwise provided by law and in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A review by a board established under and under part 51 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of enactment of the Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2025), shall be based on the records of the Coast Guard, and with respect to a member who also served in another one of the armed forces, the records of the armed forces concerned and such other evidence as may be presented to the board.section 1553 of title 10

children.heading: .—Evidence by witness
children.label: 2 .—Evidence by witness
children.text: A witness may present evidence to the board in person or by affidavit.

children.heading: .—Appearance before board
children.label: 3 .—Appearance before board
children.text: A person who requests a review under this section may appear before the board in person or by counsel or an accredited representative of an organization recognized by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs under chapter 59 of title 38.

children.heading: .—Notification
children.label: 4 .—Notification
children.text: A former member of the Coast Guard who is subject to a downgrade in discharge characterization review under subsection (a) shall be notified in writing of such proceedings, afforded the right to obtain copies of records and documents relevant to the proceedings, and the right to appear before the board in person or by counsel or an accredited representative of an organization recognized by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs under chapter 59 of title 38.
